context: Tests whether the agent produces a script with structured leveled logging (info/warn/error with timestamps to stderr) and input validation via regex before using user-supplied data in system calls. The presence of the sample CSV gives the agent concrete test data.

checklist.name: Leveled log functions
checklist.description: Script defines separate logging functions for at least three levels (e.g. log_info, log_warn, log_error or equivalents)

checklist.name: Timestamps in logs
checklist.description: Log messages include a timestamp using `date` (e.g. `date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'`)

checklist.name: Logs written to stderr
checklist.description: All logging functions write to stderr (>&2), not stdout

checklist.name: Level prefix in messages
checklist.description: Log messages include a level label such as [INFO], [WARN], or [ERROR] in the output

checklist.name: Username regex validation
checklist.description: Script validates each username against a restrictive regex pattern (e.g. ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ or similar) before using it in system commands

checklist.name: Bad username rejected
checklist.description: Script rejects or skips usernames containing special characters like `;` and logs a warning or error for them

checklist.name: Quoted variable expansions
checklist.description: Variables derived from CSV fields are quoted when used in commands (e.g. "${username}" not $username)

checklist.name: Dry-run support
checklist.description: Script implements a dry-run mode (flag or variable) that logs intended actions without executing them

checklist.name: check_command or equivalent
checklist.description: Script verifies required commands are available (e.g. useradd, awk) before processing the CSV
